kept.text:
Wolfe Research is Incremental Bullish on $DDOG Earnings:

Analyst comments: "Key thoughts: Codex, OpenAI's coding agent, is in the middle of one of the steepest adoption curves we have seen in any AI product. Last week, Sam Altman tweeted that Codex hit 4 million active users, less than two weeks after hitting 3 million, and our channel work in the developer ecosystem corroborates the underlying momentum, with numerous anecdotes of developers switching to OpenAI's Codex. The core reasons we have heard have been: 1) very strong reviews of ChatGPT 5.5, 2) capacity constraints from Anthropic significantly impacting Claude Code uptime, and 3) some pushback around Opus 4.7 relative to expectations, with some even arguing it is a downgrade from Opus 4.6.

OpenAI management has been increasingly vocal that coding is a core strategic pillar going forward, and we wanted to understand what observability stack sits behind it. Spoiler alert: We see an incremental positive for DDOG. While it is well known that OpenAI is one of DDOG's largest customers, there have been some large debates about the trajectory of that relationship over the LTM. So we decided to do some forensic sleuthing on OpenAI's public Codex repository, the only meaningful public window into their engineering stack, and we found what we believe to be strong evidence that the Codex engineering team uses DDOG specifically for tracing, which we believe will be incrementally positive.

This is the system OpenAI engineers rely on to follow a request through the Codex stack and identify what is slow or breaking. Our key finding is a recent pull request from an OpenAI engineer that embeds a live link to a Codex trace inside OpenAI's own DDOG instance, and we found multiple commit messages from OpenAI engineers over the last few months describing DDOG as where they go to view traces when debugging issues stemming from Codex.

We believe this is notable, as the single fastest-growing product surface at OpenAI today is being scaled, instrumented, and traced on top of DDOG. We continue to believe DDOG is one of the best-positioned names in our coverage to benefit from the scaling of AI infrastructure, and this finding strengthens our confidence that OpenAI is not only not churning from DDOG, but potentially expanding its spend behind growth in Codex usage.

We would caveat that our analysis is based on public repository evidence rather than a specific production configuration, and we cannot confirm exactly how DDOG usage scales with Codex end users versus internal engineering activity. On the next page, we walk through what we did, what we found, and why we think it matters."

tweet.text:
SOMEONE JUST BUILT AN OPEN SOURCE AI SYSTEM THAT AUTO-APPLIED TO 700 JOBS AND ACTUALLY GOT RESULTS.

Not a concept. Not a demo. A fully working pipeline anyone can run.

Here is what it does:

Scans thousands of job listings automatically across dozens of companies.

Reads each listing and tailors your resume to match the exact language and requirements.

Generates ATS-optimized PDFs so your application clears the algorithm before a human ever sees it.

Submits the applications automatically without you touching anything.

700 applications in the time it would take a human to manually apply to 10.

Think about what this actually means.

The job market was already brutal before AI.

Hundreds of applicants per role. ATS systems rejecting 75% before a recruiter reads a single name.

This system does not just apply faster.

It applies SMARTER.

Every resume tailored. Every keyword matched. Every application formatted to survive the filter.

The people running this while their competitors are manually updating their LinkedIn are operating in a completely different category.

Open source. Free to run. Available right now.

Bookmark this before your next job search.

Follow @cyrilXBT for more open source builds that change the game the moment they drop.
